description: Calystegia sepium is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It has a subcosmopolitan distribution throughout temperate regions of the North and South hemispheres.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Dehesiant spherical capsule with 4 seeds hidden by the bracts

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: No significant problems. Can be weedy and difficult to eradicate. It has invaded crop fields and can cause problems.

description: Convolvulus arvensis, or field bindweed, is a species of bindweed in the Convolvulaceae native to Europe and Asia. It is a rhizomatous and climbing or creeping herbaceous perennial plant with stems growing to 0.5–2 metres (1.6–6.6 ft) in length. It is usually found at ground level with small white and pink flowers.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Flowers vary in colors (including blue, white, yellow, and purple). Flowers have a few colors.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: The sweet potato or sweetpotato is a dicotyledonous plant in the morning glory family, Convolvulaceae. Its sizeable, starchy, sweet-tasting tuberous roots are used as a root vegetable, which is a staple food in parts of the world. Cultivars of the sweet potato have been bred to bear tubers with flesh and skin of various colors. Moreover, the young shoots and leaves are occasionally eaten as greens. The sweet potato and the potato are only distantly related, both being in the order Solanales.
